world.title: Three weeks in, escalating Iran war is likely slipping out of Donald Trump's hands
world.description: Middle East Conflict: Donald Trump faces a worsening Iran war crisis as energy prices surge and allies remain distant. Despite his claims of military victory, Iran's defiance chokes Gulf oil supplies, leaving the U.S. isolated and facing a prolonged conflict with no clear exit strategy. This situation risks Trump's legacy and his party's midterm prospects.

business.description:
Nvidia (NasdaqGS:NVDA) and Uber plan to launch a global Level 4 autonomous robotaxi fleet starting in 2027.
The service will run on Nvidia's DRIVE Hyperion platform and Alpamayo AI model, with phased rollouts in major US cities first.
The companies aim to expand the fleet to 28 cities worldwide by 2028 across ride hailing, logistics, and public transit use cases.

science.title: This crocodile ran like a greyhound across prehistoric Britain 200 million years ago
science.description: A newly discovered Triassic reptile from the UK looked more like a racing greyhound than a crocodile, built for speed on land. With long legs and a lightweight body, it hunted small animals in a dry, upland environment millions of years ago. Scientists identified it as a new species after spotting key differences in its fossils. It’s also a tribute to an inspiring teacher who helped spark a future scientist’s curiosity.

science.title: Atomic light could be most practical way to detect gravitational waves
science.description: Researchers proposed that passing gravitational waves can subtly retune the light emitted by atoms. Not by changing how much light they emit—but by altering how that light looks depending on direction.

ai.title: Techie regrets 2X salary job after shocking reality: ‘Working here is making me lose sleep…’
ai.description: A software professional's dream job turned into a nightmare after joining a multinational company. He encountered a chaotic work environment with reckless AI code integration, absent quality checks, and a lack of experienced leadership. This led to a cycle of broken code, immense pressure, and mental strain, sparking concerns about responsible AI usage in development.

ai.title: Delhi police hold Bihar resident for sharing AI-generated images of PM Modi
ai.description: NEW DELHI: Delhi Police have arrested a man from Bihar’s Arwal district for allegedly posting objectionable AI-generated images of Prime Minister Nare.

ai.title: AI researcher Andrej Karpathy no longer writes code; spends hours directing AI agents
ai.description: In an interview with No Priors on Friday, Karpathy said he is in a perpetual state of “AI psychosis," which refers to an intense focus on using AI tools, believing that their rapidly expanding capabilities make almost anything possible. He now spends long stretches “expressing intent” to AI systems rather than directly programming. According to Karpathy, the main bottleneck is no longer computing power but the human ability to effectively direct AI systems.

ai.title: State making questionable use of AI software for FOIA responses
